events.title: Disease in Uganda 05/15/2025
events.body:
An Ebola outbreak that began in the Democratic Republic of the Congo on May 15 has spread to neighboring Uganda, where authorities reported three new cases on Friday, bringing Uganda's total to 19 cases and two deaths. The DRC has recorded at least 381 cases and 64 deaths. The outbreak involves the Bundibugyo strain of the virus, a rare form that health officials believe had been spreading undetected for some time. It is now larger than both previous recorded outbreaks of this strain, which occurred in 2007 and 2012.

The World Health Organization and the African Union's health agency announced a $518 million response plan covering emergency coordination, testing, patient care, and community outreach through November. No approved vaccine currently exists for the Bundibugyo strain, though three vaccines are being fast-tracked for trials.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Communities in Uganda and DRC facing a spreading Ebola outbreak with rising deaths and no approved vaccine

events.title: Earthquake in India 06/05/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.9 earthquake struck about 37 kilometers west-southwest of Kyelang, a town in the Lahaul and Spiti district of northern India, on June 5, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of roughly 22 kilometers beneath the surface.

Twenty-three people submitted reports saying they felt the shaking, with some describing a strong level of motion. Kyelang sits in a remote, mountainous part of Himachal Pradesh near the Himalayan range, where earthquakes are not uncommon due to the region's active geology.

No damage or casualties have been reported from this event at this time.
events.prayer: Residents near Kyelang, India shaken by a moderate earthquake in the Himalayan region

events.title: Disease in Kenya
events.body:
Kenya's president defended his government's decision to allow the United States to construct an Ebola quarantine facility on a US air force base within the country. The facility is intended to house Americans who may have been exposed to the Ebola virus.

The announcement has triggered public protests, turning deadly this week when at least two people were killed during demonstrations against the project. The president stated that permitting the facility was the "right thing" to do, but the deaths have deepened tensions over the decision.

Ebola is a severe and often fatal disease spread through direct contact with the blood or body fluids of infected people. While no Ebola outbreak within Kenya has been reported in the source material, the planned facility has become a flashpoint for public fear and political unrest in the country.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Kenyan communities divided over an Ebola quarantine facility amid deadly unrest

events.title: Earthquake in Venezuela 06/04/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.8 earthquake struck about 32 kilometers west-southwest of El Tocuyo, Venezuela on the evening of June 4, 2026. The quake was shallow, occurring at a depth of roughly 10 kilometers, which can allow shaking to be felt more strongly at the surface.

Only a small number of felt reports have come in so far. No damage or injuries have been reported at this time, though the full picture of any local impact may not yet be known.
events.prayer: Residents near El Tocuyo, Venezuela, shaken by a shallow earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in United States 06/04/2026 — Magnitude 4
events.body:
A magnitude 3.8 earthquake struck about 11 kilometers northwest of Summerlin South, Nevada on the evening of June 4, 2026. The quake occurred at a shallow depth of about 7 kilometers, which tends to make shaking more noticeable at the surface.

More than 3,000 people reported feeling the earthquake. Shaking in the area reached a light-to-moderate level, enough for people indoors to notice movement and for objects to rattle or shift. Summerlin South is a residential community on the western edge of the Las Vegas metro area, meaning a large number of people were potentially affected.

No significant damage or injuries have been reported based on available information.
events.prayer: Residents near Las Vegas shaken by a shallow earthquake northwest of Summerlin South

events.title: Earthquake in China 06/04/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.9 earthquake struck about 57 kilometers north-northwest of Huocheng, in the Xinjiang region of northwestern China, on June 4, 2026. The quake occurred at a shallow depth of roughly 10 kilometers beneath the surface.

Shallow earthquakes tend to be felt more strongly at ground level than deeper ones. Only a small number of shaking reports have been submitted so far, and no details about damage or injuries have been confirmed at this time.
events.prayer: Residents near Huocheng, China, following a shallow earthquake northwest of the city

events.title: Disease in Congo
events.body:
An Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo is showing early signs of progress, according to the head of the World Health Organization, who said response efforts are beginning to catch up with the disease. Health officials expressed cautious hope while acknowledging that serious obstacles remain.

Key challenges include gaps in testing and disease tracking, the need for continued vaccine development, and difficulty building trust within affected communities. Without community trust, efforts to contain the outbreak and vaccinate people become much harder.

The situation remains serious, and health authorities are continuing their work across multiple fronts. The DRC has faced Ebola outbreaks before, and experience shows that consistent effort across testing, treatment, and community engagement is essential to bringing an outbreak under control.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: People in the Democratic Republic of the Congo amid an ongoing and challenging Ebola outbreak

events.title: Earthquake in United States 06/03/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck about 62 kilometers off the coast of Petrolia in northern California on June 3, 2026. The quake occurred at a shallow depth of about 10 kilometers, which tends to make shaking more noticeable at the surface.

Over 120 people reported feeling the quake, with some describing moderate to strong shaking in the area. At this level, loose objects may shift or fall, and some people may find it difficult to stand. No major damage or injuries have been reported at this time, and the overall estimated impact is considered low.

The northern California coast sits in an active seismic zone, and earthquakes of this size are not uncommon in the region. Residents are encouraged to remain alert to any aftershocks in the days ahead.
events.prayer: Residents along the northern California coast shaken by an offshore earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Indonesia 06/04/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.5 earthquake struck about 88 kilometers west of Manokwari, in the West Papua region of Indonesia, on June 4, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of roughly 38 kilometers beneath the surface.

At this magnitude and depth, significant damage is unlikely, though residents near the epicenter may have felt noticeable shaking. Indonesia sits along one of the world's most active seismic zones, and earthquakes in this region are common.

No damage or casualty reports have been issued based on available information. Residents and those working in the area are encouraged to stay alert to any updates from local authorities.
events.prayer: Communities west of Manokwari, Indonesia, following a moderate earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Vanuatu 06/03/2026 — Magnitude 6
events.body:
A magnitude 5.6 earthquake struck the Vanuatu region on June 3, 2026. The quake was shallow, occurring about 10 kilometers below the surface, which can allow stronger shaking to reach ground level.

Vanuatu sits along one of the world's most active seismic zones and regularly experiences earthquakes. Shallow events like this one can cause more noticeable shaking in nearby communities, though current information does not indicate major damage or casualties from this particular quake.

Residents in the area may have felt the tremor. Authorities typically monitor such events closely in this region given its history of seismic activity.
events.prayer: Communities in the Vanuatu region after a shallow earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in United States 06/03/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.5 earthquake struck about 65 kilometers west of Petrolia in northern California on June 3, 2026. The quake was unusually shallow, occurring less than one kilometer below the surface, which can cause stronger shaking to be felt at ground level than deeper earthquakes of the same size.

A small number of people reported feeling the quake, with some describing moderate shaking. No major damage or injuries have been reported at this time. The affected area lies along California's rugged northern coast, a region accustomed to seismic activity due to its location near major fault systems offshore.
events.prayer: Residents along the northern California coast near Petrolia after a very shallow earthquake

events.title: Disease in Congo
events.body:
An Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo has prompted health authorities in Spain to cancel an international football match between DR Congo and Chile that had been planned for 9 June. Officials in La Linea de la Concepcion, a coastal city near Gibraltar, signed a decree blocking the fixture after recommendations from regional and local health services, calling it a "precautionary measure."

The outbreak is caused by a rare strain of Ebola called Bundibugyo, for which no vaccine currently exists. The World Health Organisation has said a vaccine could take up to nine months to develop. DR Congo's players, who are based in Belgium, are not believed to have recently visited their home country, though some support staff and fans have traveled from there.

The United States has also responded by barring entry to non-Americans who have recently been in DR Congo, Uganda, or South Sudan.
events.prayer: People in eastern DR Congo amid a growing Ebola outbreak with no available vaccine

events.title: Earthquake in Fiji 06/02/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.6 earthquake struck about 232 kilometers east of Levuka, Fiji, on June 2, 2026. The quake occurred at an exceptionally deep level — roughly 618 kilometers underground — which significantly reduces the energy felt at the surface.

Because of its great depth, shaking at ground level would have been minimal across the region, and the risk of significant damage or injury is generally low for earthquakes at this depth. No damage or casualties were reported in the available information.
events.prayer: Communities in Fiji following a deep earthquake east of Levuka

events.title: Other in United States 05/30/2026
events.body:
A meteorite event accompanied by a sonic boom was reported over eastern Massachusetts. The event was recorded at the surface with no underground depth, consistent with an atmospheric occurrence rather than a ground-based earthquake. Hundreds of people submitted reports describing what they felt or heard.

Sonic booms from space rocks entering the atmosphere can shake windows, rattle buildings, and feel much like an earthquake to those nearby. While such events are dramatic, they typically cause little or no structural damage on the ground.

No injuries or significant damage have been reported at this time based on available information. Residents across the region are encouraged to check on neighbors and report any unusual findings to local authorities.
events.prayer: Residents of eastern Massachusetts startled by a meteorite and sonic boom event

events.title: Earthquake in United States 06/03/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.6 earthquake struck about 6 kilometers west-northwest of Kahaluu-Keauhou on the Big Island of Hawaii. The quake occurred in the early morning hours and was centered roughly 34 kilometers below the surface.

Over 700 people reported feeling the shaking. At that depth and magnitude, significant structural damage is unlikely, though residents in the immediate area may have felt a noticeable jolt. Hawaii sits in one of the most seismically active regions of the United States, and earthquakes of this size are not uncommon on the Big Island.

No tsunami warning was reported in connection with this event. Local residents and those serving in the area are encouraged to stay aware of any aftershocks.
events.prayer: Residents near Kahaluu-Keauhou, Hawaii shaken by a moderate earthquake

events.title: Storm in United States
events.body:
A powerful hailstorm swept through Denver, Colorado, bringing hailstones as large as golf balls and causing widespread disruption across the area. The storm created hazardous conditions on roads and affected travel throughout the region.

The severe weather was part of a broader system of storm cells moving through northeastern Colorado. Officials have not yet released details on injuries or the full extent of property damage, but the scale of disruption suggests significant impact on the community.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents of Denver and northeastern Colorado affected by damaging hailstorm

events.title: Disease in Congo
events.body:
A rare strain of Ebola known as Bundibugyo has killed nearly 250 people and infected more than 1,000 suspected cases in the Democratic Republic of Congo, with nine confirmed cases spreading into neighboring Uganda. This species has only been seen twice before, and no approved vaccine exists for it. Health officials warn the outbreak was not detected until after it had already spread through a conflict zone with limited healthcare, raising fears it could rival the devastating 2014–2016 West Africa outbreak that infected nearly 29,000 people and killed more than 11,000.

Three research groups — IAVI, Moderna, and the University of Oxford — are now racing to develop vaccines against this Ebola strain. Oxford's candidate could reach clinical trials within two to three months, while IAVI's modified version may take seven to nine months. Moderna is applying the same mRNA technology used during the COVID-19 pandemic. Funding is being provided to all three teams, with health leaders stressing that every day matters in this race against a fast-moving disease.
events.prayer: Communities in DR Congo and Uganda facing a rapidly spreading Bundibugyo Ebola outbreak with no approved vaccine

events.title: Disease in Kenya
events.body:
Two people were shot dead in the central Kenyan town of Nanyuki during protests against a US plan to establish an Ebola isolation centre at a nearby military base. One person was shot close to Laikipia Airbase during a demonstration and was brought to the local hospital by friends, where he died. The other was brought in by soldiers, already dead. Officials have not publicly confirmed the deaths, and a police spokesman said he was unaware of any fatalities.

Hundreds had marched through Nanyuki on Monday, blocking roads and burning tyres, prompting police to fire tear gas. The proposed 50-bed facility would be staffed by US medical personnel and intended to treat US citizens affected by the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo. Kenya has recorded no Ebola cases.

A court has halted the centre's opening and later ordered the government to disclose details of the plan. Despite the order, military aircraft have continued flying in and out of the base.
events.prayer: Residents of Nanyuki amid deadly protests over a proposed Ebola isolation centre

events.title: Earthquake in Japan 05/31/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.5 earthquake struck deep beneath the Bonin Islands region of Japan on May 31. The earthquake occurred at a depth of about 508 kilometers, placing it well into the lower part of the earth's mantle far below the surface.

Earthquakes at this depth are common in this part of the western Pacific, where one tectonic plate slides beneath another. Because the energy must travel such a great distance to reach the surface, deep earthquakes typically cause less shaking and damage than shallower ones of similar size.

No reports of damage or casualties were immediately available from this remote island region. Residents and those with connections to the area are encouraged to stay informed through local authorities.
events.prayer: Communities near the Bonin Islands after a deep earthquake beneath the western Pacific

events.title: Earthquake in El Salvador 06/01/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.6 earthquake struck offshore in the Pacific Ocean on June 1, about 111 kilometers southwest of Intipucá, in southern El Salvador. The quake occurred at a depth of roughly 35 kilometers beneath the seafloor.

At this magnitude and distance from shore, significant damage is unlikely, though residents in coastal communities may have felt light to moderate shaking. No reports of casualties or damage were included in available information.

El Salvador sits along the Pacific Ring of Fire and regularly experiences seismic activity. Coastal communities in this region are accustomed to earthquakes, but each event serves as a reminder of the ongoing vulnerability of people living in this seismically active part of Central America.
events.prayer: Communities near Intipucá, El Salvador, following an offshore earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in New Zealand 06/02/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.5 earthquake struck about 30 kilometers west-northwest of Titahi Bay, New Zealand, on June 2, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of roughly 67 kilometers beneath the surface.

A small number of people reported feeling the shaking, though no damage or injuries have been described in available reports. The moderate depth of the earthquake likely reduced its impact at the surface compared to shallower events of similar size.
events.prayer: Residents near Titahi Bay, New Zealand, following an offshore earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Italy 06/01/2026 — Magnitude 6
events.body:
A magnitude 6 earthquake struck about 22 kilometers west-southwest of Scarcelli, in southern Italy, on the evening of June 1, 2026. The quake originated at a depth of roughly 243 kilometers — an unusually deep tremor, which tends to spread shaking across a wide area but often causes less surface damage than shallower earthquakes of similar size.

Dozens of people reported feeling the shaking. Because of the great depth, the ground motion experienced at the surface was relatively moderate. No major damage or casualties have been reported in the source material at this time.

Residents across the region were understandably startled. Authorities and local communities will be monitoring conditions in the hours following the event.
events.prayer: Residents near Scarcelli, Italy, shaken by a magnitude 6 earthquake

events.title: Disease in Brazil
events.body:
Health authorities in Brazil are monitoring two patients in São Paulo and Rio de Janeiro for possible Ebola infection. A man from the Democratic Republic of Congo showed symptoms including fever in São Paulo, while a Belgian man who arrived from Uganda showed symptoms including cough, chills, and diarrhea in Rio de Janeiro. Test results are expected next week. If confirmed, these would be the first Ebola cases outside Africa since the current outbreak began.

Both patients have already been diagnosed with other illnesses — the man in São Paulo tested positive for meningitis and is in serious condition, while the patient in Rio tested positive for malaria. Officials say these diagnoses do not rule out Ebola.

The outbreak, caused by a rare strain called Bundibugyo, has now recorded more than 1,000 suspected cases and at least 246 deaths in DR Congo. Uganda has also reported nine confirmed cases. This strain has no proven vaccine and kills roughly one in three people infected.
events.prayer: Two patients in Brazil under watch for Ebola as the outbreak spreads beyond Africa

events.title: Flood in Laos 05/20/2025
events.body:
Seven villagers entered a narrow cave system in Laos's mountainous Xaysomboun province on May 20 to search for gold, but flash floods trapped them inside. Five have since been rescued — four of them emerging on Saturday after water levels dropped enough for them to free themselves without direct help from rescuers.

Two men remain trapped deeper inside the cave, which is so narrow it measures around 50 centimeters wide in some places. Survivors recovering in hospital have been giving rescuers detailed descriptions of the cave's deeper sections to help plan a new search effort. A drainage pump used to lower water levels broke down, temporarily blocking the passage used in Saturday's rescue.

On Sunday, heavy rain caused water to flow back into the cave entrance, forcing a pause in operations. Specialist divers from Thailand, Indonesia, France, and Australia are among those assisting. Search efforts were set to resume Monday.
events.prayer: Two men still trapped deep inside a flooded cave in central Laos

events.title: Disease in Congo
events.body:
Health authorities in the Democratic Republic of Congo are marking a hopeful moment after five patients recovered from Ebola and were discharged from hospital. Four of them were nurses treated in Bunia, the main city of Ituri province, which sits at the center of the outbreak. A fifth, a laboratory worker, was discharged the previous week. Officials used the occasion to encourage people to seek care early if they show symptoms, stressing that recovery is possible.

The outbreak has now reached more than 1,000 suspected cases and at least 246 deaths across the country. Neighboring Uganda has also reported nine confirmed cases and one death. This strain of Ebola, known as Bundibugyo, has no approved vaccine, though some are in development.

Tensions have complicated the response in some areas, with residents attacking health centers over burial rules that prevent families from handling the bodies of those who may have died from the virus. Officials are urging communities to work alongside medical staff, saying local trust and cooperation are essential to ending the outbreak.
events.prayer: Communities across DR Congo and Uganda enduring the ongoing Ebola outbreak with over 1,000 suspected cases

events.title: Storm in Japan 05/27/2026 — Cat 1
events.body:
Tropical Storm Jangmi swept through the Northwest Pacific from late May into early June 2026, bringing maximum wind speeds of around 139 km/h. The storm affected Japan, where roughly 1.27 million people were exposed to Category 1 strength winds or higher.

Japan is considered to have relatively low vulnerability to storms of this type, given its infrastructure and emergency systems. Even so, storms of this intensity can disrupt communities, damage property, and put lives at risk, particularly in coastal and low-lying areas.

Residents across affected areas were likely dealing with strong winds, heavy rain, and possible flooding during the storm's passage. Local authorities and communities would have been working to protect people and limit damage throughout the storm's active period.
events.prayer: Over a million people in Japan in the path of Tropical Storm Jangmi

events.title: Earthquake in New Zealand 05/31/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck the Kermadec Islands region on May 31, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of about 99 kilometers beneath the ocean floor, which is considered a moderately deep earthquake.

At this depth, shaking at the surface is generally weaker than earthquakes closer to the surface. The Kermadec Islands are a remote, largely uninhabited island chain northeast of New Zealand's main islands. No reports of damage or casualties are available from the source material.
events.prayer: People in the remote Kermadec Islands region following an undersea earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Australia 06/01/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck the remote Macquarie Island region in the southern ocean on June 1, 2026. The quake occurred at a shallow depth of about 10 kilometers beneath the seafloor.

Macquarie Island is a remote Australian territory located roughly halfway between New Zealand and Antarctica. The area is extremely isolated, with no permanent civilian population — only a small number of researchers stationed there at any given time. No damage or injuries have been reported.

Because of its shallow depth and remote location, the quake was unlikely to cause significant harm, though shallow earthquakes can produce stronger ground shaking than deeper ones.
events.prayer: Isolated communities near Macquarie Island following a shallow ocean earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Chile 05/31/2026 — Magnitude 6
events.body:
A magnitude 6 earthquake struck about 33 kilometers northwest of Valparaíso, Chile on May 31, 2026. The quake originated at a depth of about 25 kilometers beneath the surface. More than 150 people submitted reports saying they felt the shaking.

Valparaíso is a densely populated coastal city and one of Chile's most important port areas. Earthquakes are common along Chile's coast due to the active fault systems running the length of the country. At this magnitude and depth, shaking was felt across a wide area, though no significant damage or casualties have been reported at this time.
events.prayer: Residents near Valparaíso, Chile shaken by a magnitude 6 earthquake

events.title: Volcano in Indonesia 05/30/2026
events.body:
Mount Merapi in West Sumatra's Tanah Datar District erupted, sending a column of ash roughly 2 kilometres into the sky. Videos of the eruption circulated widely, showing the scale of the ash plume rising from the volcano.

Authorities have maintained a 3-kilometre exclusion zone around the volcano since a previous eruption in 2023. That restricted area remains in place following this latest activity, keeping nearby communities from returning to the area closest to the summit.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents near Mount Merapi in West Sumatra living under threat of ongoing volcanic eruption

events.title: Flood in Syria
events.body:
Rescue workers have pulled stranded farmers from floodwaters in eastern Syria after the Euphrates River burst its banks. The flooding has affected several areas in the region, with Deir Az Zor among the hardest hit.

In that area, rising waters caused a bridge to collapse, cutting off communities from one another. Rescuers worked to reach people trapped by the floodwaters, though the full extent of damage and the number of those affected remain unclear.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Farmers and cut-off communities in eastern Syria amid Euphrates River flooding

events.title: Other in Australia
events.body:
A mouse plague is sweeping through large parts of Australia, hitting Western Australia and South Australia hardest. Farmers are reporting thousands of mice per hectare — well above the 8,000–10,000 estimated in some canola fields — eating seeds directly from the ground overnight and destroying crops before they can take root. Farmers describe hearing and smelling the mice in their homes, ceilings, and air conditioning units around the clock.

The plague is being called worse than a similar outbreak in 2021, which devastated parts of New South Wales and Queensland. This time, a record harvest last year left large amounts of spilled grain across fields, and summer rains produced new plant growth — together creating ideal conditions for mouse populations to explode.

Farmers are spending hundreds of thousands of dollars on bait and replanting, even as fuel and fertiliser costs have doubled in recent months. Autumn is the most critical planting season for grain growers, and many fields are at risk if baiting cannot keep pace with seeding.
events.prayer: Australian farmers in Western Australia and South Australia enduring a devastating mouse plague threatening their livelihoods

events.title: Flood in Laos 05/20/2025
events.body:
Four men trapped inside a flooded cave in Laos for ten days were freed on Saturday, rescuers announced. They are among seven villagers who entered narrow underground tunnels searching for gold on May 20, only to become trapped when flash floods swept through the remote mountain area of central Xaysomboun province. A fifth man had been rescued the day before, but two men remain missing.

The cave system is extremely tight in places, with some chambers only about 50 centimeters wide. Rescue teams had initially tried to pump out the floodwaters, but that plan failed. Specialist divers from Thailand, Indonesia, France, and Australia flew in to help, with plans discussed to teach the trapped men to scuba dive out.

Video from the scene showed the four men emerging to cheers from rescuers and onlookers. The men were placed on stretchers, wrapped in foil blankets, and taken to a medical tent for treatment.
events.prayer: Seven gold seekers trapped in a flooded Laos cave, two still missing after ten days

events.title: Disease in Congo
events.body:
An Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo has grown to more than 1,000 suspected cases and at least 246 deaths, with medical charity MSF warning the situation is "deeply alarming." Officials say no previous Ebola outbreak has recorded so many cases so quickly after being declared. The hardest-hit area is Ituri Province in eastern Congo, where the disease is spreading faster than containment efforts can keep up. Neighboring Uganda has also reported nine confirmed cases and one death.

Hundreds of samples remain untested, meaning the true scale of the outbreak is unknown. Containment efforts are being slowed by ongoing conflict in the region, as well as border and airport closures. The current strain — known as Bundibugyo — has no proven vaccine and kills roughly one in three people infected.

In a separate development, health officials in Brazil are investigating a suspected case involving a man who recently returned from Congo, now being held in isolation in São Paulo state.
events.prayer: Communities in eastern DR Congo and neighboring Uganda amid a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak

events.title: Storm in Japan 05/27/2026 — Cat 1
events.body:
Typhoon Jangmi brought strong winds and heavy rain to Japan from late May into the final days of the month. The storm reached maximum wind speeds of around 130 kilometers per hour, placing it in the Category 1 range on the typhoon scale.

An estimated 2.8 million people were exposed to Category 1 wind speeds or higher during the storm's passage. Japan's history of typhoon preparedness generally helps communities respond, though storms of this strength still pose serious risks to life, property, and infrastructure.

The storm remained active over the northwestern Pacific from May 27 through May 31. Residents in affected areas faced dangerous winds and the conditions that typically accompany strong tropical systems, including heavy rainfall and coastal hazards.
events.prayer: Millions of residents across Japan bracing for Typhoon Jangmi's dangerous winds and rain

events.title: Earthquake in New Zealand 05/29/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.5 earthquake struck about 46 kilometers east-southeast of Witherlea, New Zealand on May 29, 2026. The earthquake occurred at a depth of roughly 17 kilometers beneath the surface.

No damage or casualty information was included in the available report. Earthquakes of this size can be felt across a wide area and may cause minor disruption, though significant damage is less common at this magnitude. Residents in the region are likely familiar with seismic activity, as New Zealand sits along active fault zones.
events.prayer: Communities near Witherlea, New Zealand shaken by a moderate earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Papua New Guinea 05/30/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck about 81 kilometers southeast of Lae, Papua New Guinea, on May 30, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of roughly 69 kilometers beneath the surface.

At that depth, much of the energy is absorbed before reaching the ground above, which can reduce shaking felt by people on the surface. No damage or casualties have been reported from this event based on available information.

Papua New Guinea sits in one of the world's most seismically active regions, and earthquakes of this size are not uncommon there. Residents in the area are encouraged to remain alert, as aftershocks can follow even moderate events.
events.prayer: Communities southeast of Lae, Papua New Guinea, after a moderate earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Fiji 05/31/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck south of the Fiji Islands on May 31, 2026. The quake originated at an exceptional depth of about 486 kilometers underground, placing it among the deepest category of earthquakes.

At such extreme depths, seismic energy typically spreads across a very wide area but causes significantly less surface shaking than shallower quakes. Damage and injury risk are generally much lower for events this deep. No specific impacts or casualties were reported in the available information.

The region south of Fiji sits along an active tectonic zone in the southwestern Pacific, where deep earthquakes are not uncommon.
events.prayer: Communities near the Fiji Islands following a deep ocean earthquake

events.title: Other in United States 05/30/2026
events.body:
A loud sonic boom was reported over eastern Massachusetts, startling residents across the area. The event was recorded at the surface with no measurable depth, indicating it originated in the air rather than underground. Nearly 400 people submitted felt reports describing the disturbance.

Authorities have not yet identified the source of the boom. No damage or injuries have been reported at this time, but the sudden, unexplained noise caused widespread concern among those who experienced it.
events.prayer: Residents of eastern Massachusetts shaken and unsettled by an unexplained sonic boom

events.title: Disease in Congo
events.body:
Two weeks into a new Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of Congo, health officials now count 906 suspected cases and 223 suspected deaths. The outbreak is spreading rapidly, raising serious concern among international health workers.

The World Health Organization is on the ground responding to the crisis. One of the defining features of Ebola is how easily it spreads to those who are simply trying to help — caregivers, family members, and health workers face significant risk of infection while looking after the sick.

The situation remains serious, with hundreds of families affected and communities under stress. Health teams are working to contain the spread, but the outbreak continues to grow. People across the country and around the world are watching closely as responders work to stop further loss of life.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Hundreds of families in the Democratic Republic of Congo facing a rapidly spreading Ebola outbreak with rising deaths

events.title: Storm in Japan 05/27/2026 — Cat 1
events.body:
Tropical Storm Jangmi swept through the northwestern Pacific from May 27 to May 29, 2026, bringing maximum wind speeds of around 148 kilometers per hour. The storm affected Japan, with winds strong enough to be classified as Category 1 or higher reaching an estimated 1.6 million people.

Storms of this strength can bring dangerous winds, heavy rainfall, and flooding to coastal and inland areas alike. Residents in affected areas faced the threat of damage to homes, disrupted transportation, and potential flooding.

Japan has well-developed systems for responding to typhoons and tropical storms, but the large number of people in the storm's path means many communities will have needed to take shelter and prepare for disruptions in the days the storm was active.
events.prayer: Around 1.6 million people in Japan facing dangerous winds, heavy rainfall, and flooding from Tropical Storm Jangmi

events.title: Disease in Congo
events.body:
A deadly return of Ebola is spreading through the eastern part of the Democratic Republic of the Congo, a region already struggling with serious instability and armed conflict. The head of the World Health Organization traveled to the country to respond to the growing crisis.

The combination of violence and disease is making it extremely difficult for health teams to contain the outbreak. Conflict in the area limits access to sick communities and disrupts the kind of coordinated response that Ebola requires to bring under control.

The WHO chief also called for a ceasefire, recognizing that the fighting itself is fueling the health emergency. Without greater safety and stability in the region, efforts to stop Ebola from spreading further face serious obstacles.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Communities in the Democratic Republic of the Congo facing simultaneous Ebola outbreak and armed conflict

events.title: Earthquake in Fiji 05/29/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.6 earthquake struck south of the Fiji Islands on May 29, 2026. The quake was very deep, occurring about 499 kilometers underground, which is considered an exceptionally deep earthquake.

At that depth, the energy released travels through a large volume of rock before reaching the surface, meaning shaking is typically spread over a wide area but felt less intensely than a shallower quake of the same size. No damage reports are included in the available information.

Deep earthquakes in this part of the southwestern Pacific are not unusual, as the region sits along active tectonic boundaries. Residents and those serving in nearby communities are encouraged to stay aware of local updates from official sources.
events.prayer: Fiji residents and communities on alert following a deep 4.6-magnitude earthquake south of the islands

events.title: Heatwave in Portugal
events.body:
A powerful heatwave has swept across western Europe, with Portugal recording its hottest May temperature ever — 40.3C in the central town of Mora — breaking a record that had stood since 2001. Italy issued red heatwave alerts for Rome, Florence, Bologna, Brescia, and Turin, warning of health risks even for healthy people. Temperatures in Rome reached 32C, and Madrid is expected to hit 35C over the weekend.

France has been particularly affected, with 17 departments under orange alert and Paris reaching 33C. A primary school in the Landes region recorded 53C inside, forcing it to close. Despite widespread concern from teachers and unions, students are still sitting national exams in the heat, with some teachers reportedly bringing their own fans and screwdrivers to pry windows open.

The heat is caused by a "heat dome" of trapped warm air and is forecast to linger into the weekend before gradually easing. Scientists note that climate change is making such heatwaves more frequent and more intense across the continent.
events.prayer: Residents across Portugal and western Europe enduring record-breaking May heatwave with serious health risks

events.title: Earthquake in United States 05/28/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.7 earthquake struck about 154 kilometers southwest of Merizo Village, Guam, on May 28, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of about 36 kilometers beneath the seafloor in the western Pacific Ocean.

No damage or injuries have been reported from this event based on available information. Earthquakes of this size in the region are not uncommon, as Guam sits along the Pacific Ring of Fire where seismic activity is frequent.

Residents in the area are encouraged to stay alert for any follow-up shaking.
events.prayer: Residents of Guam on alert after a magnitude 4.7 offshore earthquake with no reported damage

events.title: Earthquake in Indonesia 05/29/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A moderate earthquake struck off the coast of Sumatra, about 73 kilometers southeast of Sinabang, Indonesia. The quake measured magnitude 4.7 and occurred in the early morning hours.

The earthquake originated at a depth of about 28 kilometers beneath the seafloor. At this size and depth, significant damage is unlikely, though residents near the epicenter may have felt noticeable shaking.

Sinabang is the main town on Simeulue Island, a remote area in Aceh province that sits along one of the world's most active earthquake zones. No damage or casualty reports were included in the available information.
events.prayer: Coastal communities in Indonesia near a moderate earthquake zone experiencing seismic activity and uncertainty

events.title: Landslide in Laos
events.body:
Five of seven villagers trapped inside a flooded cave in central Laos have been found alive after more than a week underground. The group entered a cave in Xaisomboun Province last week, but heavy rain and a landslide quickly blocked their only way out. Rescue teams from Laos and Thailand worked together to reach them, with the five survivors found in good health and high spirits.

Two people remain missing, and search efforts are continuing. Reaching the cave requires a steep hike of about four kilometres, and the entrance itself is narrow and rocky — barely wide enough for one person to pass through, making the operation especially difficult.

Locals reportedly enter the cave searching for gold, despite repeated safety warnings from authorities. The Thai rescue team included a diver who took part in the well-known 2018 cave rescue in northern Thailand, where twelve boys and their football coach were brought out alive.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Two missing villagers in Laos still unaccounted for after a landslide trapped a group inside a flooded cave

events.title: Earthquake in Tonga 05/27/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck in the ocean about 280 kilometers west-northwest of Neiafu, Tonga, on May 27, 2026. The quake was shallow, occurring only about 10 kilometers below the seafloor, which can cause stronger shaking to be felt at the surface compared to deeper earthquakes.

No details about damage, injuries, or a tsunami warning were included in the available information. Because the epicenter was far out at sea and relatively distant from populated areas, the impact on communities may be limited, but the shallow depth means those nearest the area could have felt noticeable shaking.

Tonga sits in one of the most seismically active regions of the world, and earthquakes in this zone are common. Residents and those serving in the islands are encouraged to stay aware of local updates.
events.prayer: Residents of Tonga experiencing shallow offshore earthquake and awaiting news of any damage or impact

events.title: Heatwave in France
events.body:
A heat dome settled over Western Europe this week, pushing temperatures far above normal for May. In Paris, the heat reached 34°C (93°F), with people rushing to find shade in conditions that felt more like the height of summer. France's national weather service said the unusual pattern was producing temperatures 10 to 15 degrees Celsius above the seasonal average.

The heat caught many off guard. A visitor from Australia told reporters he had chosen May specifically to avoid the intense summer heat of June, July, and August, only to find the same conditions waiting for him.

Separately, the source also notes that storms named Marta and Leonardo caused widespread flooding in Spain, displacing thousands of people — a reminder that extreme weather was affecting multiple parts of the region at the same time.
events.prayer: Millions of residents across France enduring a dangerous early-season heatwave with temperatures far above normal

events.title: Flood in Laos
events.body:
Rescue teams from Laos and Thailand have pulled five villagers out alive after they were trapped for a week inside a flooded cave in the central province of Xaysomboun, about 120 kilometers north of the capital Vientiane. The seven villagers had entered the cave to search for gold and wildlife but became trapped when heavy rain and landslides blocked the entrance. Two others who were with them remain missing.

The cave system is extremely narrow in places — some chambers only about 50 centimeters wide — and rescue divers had to crawl through muddy, nearly fully flooded passageways stretching hundreds of meters. A specialist diver described navigating flood waters, collapse hazards, and the risk of contaminated air throughout the operation.

The five survivors were located roughly 300 meters from the cave's exit. Rescuers say the search for the two missing villagers is continuing.
events.prayer: Five survivors rescued and two villagers still missing after being trapped in a flooded cave in Laos

events.title: Storm in Japan 05/27/2026 — Cat 1
events.body:
Tropical Cyclone Jangmi formed in the northwestern Pacific Ocean on May 27, 2026, with maximum wind speeds reaching about 167 kilometers per hour. The storm is classified as a tropical depression in terms of its official category, though its winds are strong enough to place it at hurricane-force intensity.

Japan is the country most directly in the storm's path. At the time of reporting, no populated areas had been hit by Category 1 or stronger winds, meaning the most damaging winds had not yet reached land or had passed without striking densely settled areas.

Residents in coastal and island communities near the storm's track are advised to follow local authorities and stay informed as conditions can change quickly with Pacific storm systems.
events.prayer: Coastal communities in Japan bracing for a powerful tropical cyclone with hurricane-force winds

events.title: Earthquake in New Zealand 05/26/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck the Kermadec Islands region, a remote stretch of the southwestern Pacific Ocean northeast of New Zealand. The quake was shallow, occurring about 10 kilometers below the surface, which can increase the strength of shaking felt at ground level.

The Kermadec Islands region is one of the most seismically active areas on Earth, sitting along a major tectonic boundary where the Pacific Plate dives beneath the Australian Plate. Earthquakes in this remote area are common.

No population centers are located close to the epicenter, and no damage or casualties have been reported. Residents of northern New Zealand and nearby island groups were not likely affected by this event.
events.prayer: Remote New Zealand coastal region experiencing seismic activity near a major tectonic boundary

events.title: Earthquake in Japan 05/27/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.6 earthquake struck offshore Japan on May 27, 2026, centered about 84 kilometers south-southeast of Hiroo, a coastal town on the island of Hokkaido. The quake occurred at a depth of about 38 kilometers beneath the seafloor.

At this size and distance from shore, the earthquake was likely felt only lightly by people nearest the coast, and no major damage would be expected. Offshore earthquakes in this region are common due to the active tectonic setting where the Pacific Plate meets the Eurasian Plate.

No reports of damage or injuries were included in the available information.
events.prayer: Coastal communities in Japan unsettled by a magnitude 4.6 offshore earthquake near Hokkaido

events.title: Earthquake in Papua New Guinea 05/27/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.8 earthquake struck about 58 kilometers north of Finschhafen, Papua New Guinea on May 27, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of about 76 kilometers, which helped reduce the energy felt at the surface.

At that depth and size, significant damage is unlikely, though people close to the epicenter may have felt noticeable shaking. Papua New Guinea sits in one of the most seismically active regions in the world, and tremors of this size are not uncommon there.

No damage or casualty reports are available from the source at this time.
events.prayer: Communities in Papua New Guinea rattled by a moderate earthquake with no confirmed damage reports yet

events.title: Earthquake in Chile 05/25/2026 — Magnitude 7
events.body:
A magnitude 6.9 earthquake struck about 29 kilometers northeast of Calama, Chile on May 25, 2026. The quake was centered about 109 kilometers deep, which helped limit the intensity of shaking felt at the surface. Dozens of people reported feeling the tremor.

Calama is a city in northern Chile's Atacama Desert, a region no stranger to seismic activity due to its location along the Andes. The depth of this quake — well over 100 kilometers underground — generally reduces the risk of severe surface damage compared to shallower events. No major casualties or damage reports were immediately available from the source material.

Communities in the area are encouraged to check on neighbors and follow guidance from local authorities as any aftershocks may follow in the coming hours and days.
events.prayer: Residents across northern Chile shaken by a magnitude 7 earthquake and awaiting news of damage or aftershocks

events.title: Earthquake in Timor Leste 05/25/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A moderate earthquake struck in waters northeast of Lospalos, Timor-Leste on May 25, 2026. The quake had a magnitude of about 4.9 and was centered roughly 224 kilometers offshore.

The earthquake occurred at a depth of about 139 kilometers, which is considered deep. Greater depth generally means shaking is felt more widely but is less intense at the surface compared to a shallow quake of the same size.

No damage or injury reports are included in the available information. Due to the remote offshore location and the depth of the event, significant impact to communities is less likely, though people in coastal areas of Timor-Leste and nearby islands may have felt some shaking.
events.prayer: Coastal communities in Timor-Leste experiencing seismic activity from an offshore magnitude 5 earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Papua New Guinea 05/25/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck about 104 kilometers east-southeast of Kokopo, Papua New Guinea on May 25, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of about 67 kilometers, which helped reduce the energy felt at the surface.

No damage or injuries have been reported at this time. At that depth, shaking is typically spread over a wider area but tends to be less intense than a shallower quake of the same size.

Papua New Guinea sits along one of the world's most active seismic zones, and earthquakes in this region are common. Residents in the area are accustomed to tremors, but each event serves as a reminder of the ongoing risk faced by communities across this part of the Pacific.
events.prayer: Communities in Papua New Guinea living with ongoing seismic risk after a magnitude 5 earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Solomon Islands 05/22/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck about 156 kilometers west of Lata in the Solomon Islands. The quake was shallow, occurring about 10 kilometers below the surface, which can cause stronger shaking at ground level than deeper earthquakes.

No damage or injuries have been reported at this time. The Solomon Islands sit in a seismically active part of the Pacific, and earthquakes in this range are not uncommon for the region.
events.prayer: Communities in the Solomon Islands shaken by a shallow magnitude 5 earthquake with damage still being assessed

events.title: Earthquake in Samoa 05/23/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.9 earthquake struck in the ocean about 141 kilometers south-southwest of Gataivai, Samoa, on May 23, 2026. The quake was shallow, occurring only about 10 kilometers below the surface, which can allow shaking to be felt more strongly at ground level.

No details about damage or injuries were included in the available information. Because the epicenter was well out to sea and at a moderate magnitude, significant impact to populated areas may be limited, but coastal communities in Samoa could have felt the tremor.

Shallow offshore earthquakes in this region are not unusual, as Samoa sits within an active seismic zone in the South Pacific. Residents in the area are generally familiar with earthquake activity, though any tremor near island communities warrants attentiveness.
events.prayer: Coastal communities in Samoa shaken by a shallow offshore earthquake with uncertain damage

events.title: Earthquake in Philippines 05/24/2026 — Magnitude 5.5
events.body:
A magnitude 5.5 earthquake struck about 13 kilometers east of Cortes in the Philippines on May 24, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of about 34 kilometers below the surface, which is considered moderate depth.

Reports from people in the area indicate noticeable shaking was felt locally. At that magnitude and depth, some minor damage to older or weaker structures is possible, though initial assessments suggested the overall impact was limited.

Residents in the affected area are encouraged to inspect buildings for cracks or structural concerns before returning inside. Aftershocks are common following earthquakes of this size, so people in the region should remain cautious in the hours and days ahead.
events.prayer: Residents in the Philippines assessing structural damage and bracing for aftershocks following a magnitude 5.5 earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in United States 05/23/2026 — Magnitude 6
events.body:
A magnitude 6 earthquake struck near the southern coast of Hawaii's Big Island on May 23, 2026, centered about 13 kilometers south of Honaunau-Napoopoo. The quake occurred at a depth of roughly 23 kilometers beneath the surface — a moderate depth that allowed strong shaking to reach communities across the region. More than 7,000 people submitted reports describing what they felt, indicating that shaking was widely experienced across the island.

Hawaii sits in a zone of frequent seismic activity due to volcanic and tectonic forces beneath the Pacific. A magnitude 6 earthquake at this depth can cause structural damage, especially to older buildings, and may trigger rockfalls on steep terrain common to the Big Island's landscape.

Local residents and visitors are encouraged to check on neighbors and inspect buildings carefully before re-entering any structure that may have been affected.
events.prayer: Communities across Hawaii's Big Island shaken by a magnitude 6 earthquake with potential structural damage and rockfalls

events.title: Earthquake in Chile 05/22/1960 — Magnitude 10
events.body:
On May 22, 1960, the most powerful earthquake ever recorded struck southern Chile. The quake reached a magnitude of 9.5 — a force unlike anything measured before or since. It killed 1,655 people and left approximately 2 million others without homes.

The destruction did not stop at Chile's shores. The earthquake triggered a massive tsunami that traveled across the Pacific Ocean, killing more than 230 additional people in Hawaii, Japan, and the Philippines. Communities thousands of miles from the epicenter were swept by waves with little warning.

This event remains a milestone in how the world understands earthquake and tsunami risk. It changed the way scientists, governments, and emergency planners think about preparedness for large-scale disasters along coastlines worldwide.
events.prayer: Millions in Chile left homeless and communities across the Pacific devastated by catastrophic earthquake and tsunami

events.title: Earthquake in Philippines 05/21/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.7 earthquake struck about 104 kilometers southeast of Pondaguitan in the southern Philippines on May 21, 2026. The quake was centered at a depth of about 132 kilometers, which is considered a deep earthquake. At that depth, shaking at the surface is generally less intense than it would be from a shallower event of the same size.

No damage or injuries were reported in the source material. The southern Philippines sits within an active seismic zone, and earthquakes of this type occur regularly in the region.
events.prayer: Communities in the southern Philippines living in an active seismic zone amid recurring earthquake activity

events.title: Earthquake in Indonesia 05/22/2026 — Magnitude 6
events.body:
A magnitude 5.9 earthquake struck underwater about 113 kilometers east-southeast of Bitung, Indonesia, in the early morning hours of May 22, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of about 35 kilometers beneath the seafloor.

No immediate reports of casualties or significant damage have been noted, and the estimated impact appears low at this time. The relatively moderate depth means shaking may have been felt across a wide area of the surrounding region, though the offshore location likely reduced the effect on populated areas.

Residents in coastal communities near Bitung and the North Sulawesi region should remain attentive to any updates from local authorities.
events.prayer: Coastal communities in Indonesia on alert after a moderate offshore earthquake near populated areas

events.title: Earthquake in Solomon Islands 05/22/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck about 160 kilometers west of Lata in the Solomon Islands. The quake was shallow, occurring only about 10 kilometers beneath the surface, which can cause stronger shaking to be felt at ground level compared to deeper earthquakes.

No damage or casualties have been reported at this time, and the estimated impact appears to be low. The remote location of the epicenter, well off the coast, likely limited any effect on populated areas.

The Solomon Islands sit in one of the most seismically active regions in the world, and earthquakes are common there. Residents and those working in the area are encouraged to stay alert to any updates from local authorities.
events.prayer: Residents of the Solomon Islands on alert after a shallow magnitude 5 offshore earthquake

events.title: Earthquake in Papua New Guinea 05/22/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.8 earthquake struck about 45 kilometers east of Lae, Papua New Guinea on May 22, 2026. The earthquake was centered roughly 70 kilometers underground, which is considered a moderately deep quake. Deeper earthquakes generally cause less surface damage than shallow ones, though shaking can still be felt across a wide area.

Lae is Papua New Guinea's second-largest city and a major port and industrial hub. No damage reports or casualties were immediately available from the source material. Residents in the area may have felt moderate shaking.

Papua New Guinea sits along the Pacific Ring of Fire and experiences earthquakes regularly. Communities there are no strangers to seismic activity, though each event is a reminder of the region's vulnerability.
events.prayer: Residents of Papua New Guinea near a major port city experiencing moderate shaking from a 4.8 earthquake

events.title: Flood in China 05/21/2026
events.body:
Heavy flooding has struck southern China, sweeping away cars, destroying at least one bridge, and leaving streets underwater. Torrential rain triggered the disaster, killing at least 25 people and forcing residents to evacuate across multiple areas of the region.

Authorities have been working to move people to safety as floodwaters continue to cause widespread damage. The full extent of destruction is still being assessed.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera
events.prayer: Residents across southern China displaced and grieving after deadly floods destroying homes and infrastructure

events.title: Earthquake in Iran 05/21/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck about 61 kilometers west of Bandar Abbas, Iran, on May 21, 2026. The quake was shallow, occurring about 10 kilometers beneath the surface, which can cause stronger shaking to be felt at ground level compared to deeper earthquakes.

Bandar Abbas is a major port city on the Strait of Hormuz in southern Iran. Shallow earthquakes in this region can be felt widely across the surrounding area, including coastal communities and nearby towns.

No damage or casualty information was included in the available report. Residents in the area are encouraged to stay alert to any official guidance from local authorities in the aftermath.
events.prayer: Residents in southern Iran rattled by a shallow magnitude 5 earthquake near a major coastal city

events.title: Earthquake in Turkey — Magnitude 6
events.body:
A magnitude 5.6 earthquake struck the Battalgazi district of Malatya province in southeastern Turkey on a Wednesday morning. The quake was shallow, about 7 kilometers deep, and sent residents rushing outdoors in panic. Schools were evacuated and remained closed for the rest of the day across Malatya and several neighboring provinces, including Adiyaman, Elazig, Tunceli, and Sanliurfa.

At least 74 people sought medical care. About half were treated for panic-related complaints or minor injuries and discharged; 18 remained in hospital for observation. Authorities continued inspecting the area for structural damage.

The tremor hit a region that was devastated just over two years ago by two powerful earthquakes — both above magnitude 7 — on February 6, 2023. Those quakes killed more than 55,000 people in Turkey and thousands more in neighboring Syria, leaving communities across 11 provinces still in recovery.
events.prayer: Residents across southeastern Turkey shaken by earthquake, with dozens hospitalized and communities still recovering from 2023 disaster

events.title: Earthquake in Vanuatu 05/20/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.5 earthquake struck about 25 kilometers northeast of Isangel, on Tanna Island in Vanuatu, on May 20, 2026. The quake originated very deep underground — about 260 kilometers down — which typically reduces the amount of shaking felt at the surface.

At that depth, serious surface damage is less likely than with shallower earthquakes of similar size. However, Vanuatu sits in one of the world's most active seismic zones, and communities there are accustomed to frequent tremors.

No damage or injuries were reported in the available information.
events.prayer: Communities in Vanuatu experiencing frequent seismic activity and ongoing earthquake risk

events.title: Earthquake in Vanuatu 05/19/2026 — Magnitude 6
events.body:
A moderate earthquake struck about 51 kilometers southwest of Port-Vila, the capital of Vanuatu, on May 19, 2026. The quake had a magnitude of 5.7 and was centered about 27 kilometers below the surface — a relatively shallow depth that can make shaking more noticeable at ground level.

Vanuatu sits along one of the most seismically active zones in the Pacific, and earthquakes of this size are not uncommon in the region. A small number of people reported feeling the shaking, though no major damage or casualties have been confirmed from this event.

Residents in and around Port-Vila are encouraged to stay alert, as aftershocks can follow moderate earthquakes in this part of the world.
events.prayer: Residents of Vanuatu shaken by a moderate earthquake and awaiting possible aftershocks

events.title: Earthquake in Indonesia 05/20/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.6 earthquake struck the sea about 131 kilometers west-northwest of Ternate, Indonesia on May 20, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of about 54 kilometers beneath the seafloor.

At this size and distance from shore, significant damage is unlikely, though residents in the area may have felt some shaking. Ternate is a densely populated island city in the North Maluku province, known for its position near active tectonic fault zones.

No reports of damage or casualties were immediately available from the source material. The region regularly experiences seismic activity due to its location along major tectonic plate boundaries in the western Pacific.
events.prayer: Residents in Indonesia near active fault zones experiencing recurring seismic activity and uncertainty

events.title: Earthquake in Papua New Guinea 05/19/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A moderate earthquake struck about 79 kilometers south-southeast of Kimbe, Papua New Guinea on May 19, 2026. The quake had a magnitude of 4.7 and occurred at a depth of about 60 kilometers beneath the surface.

At that depth, shaking at the surface is generally less intense than a shallower quake of the same size, though people in nearby communities may still have felt it. No damage or casualties have been reported in the available information.

Papua New Guinea sits in one of the world's most seismically active regions, and earthquakes in this area are common. Those serving or living in the region around Kimbe are encouraged to stay aware of local conditions and official guidance.
events.prayer: Communities in Papua New Guinea near a moderate earthquake zone facing ongoing seismic activity and uncertainty

events.title: Earthquake in Japan 05/19/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.5 earthquake struck about 25 kilometers northwest of Takayama, Japan on May 19, 2026. The quake was very deep — roughly 262 kilometers below the surface — which typically means shaking is felt over a wide area but is less intense at ground level than a shallower quake of the same size.

No damage or injuries have been reported from this event. Deep earthquakes like this one rarely cause significant harm, though residents in the region may have felt light to moderate shaking.

Takayama is a historic city in the mountainous Gifu Prefecture of central Japan. Japan sits on one of the most seismically active regions in the world, and small to moderate earthquakes are common throughout the country.
events.prayer: Residents across Japan experiencing seismic activity following a deep magnitude 4.5 earthquake with widespread light shaking

events.title: Earthquake in Vanuatu 05/19/2026 — Magnitude 6
events.body:
A magnitude 5.8 earthquake struck about 49 kilometers south-southwest of Port-Vila, the capital of Vanuatu, in the early morning hours of May 19, 2026. The quake occurred at a depth of about 37 kilometers beneath the seabed.

Vanuatu sits along the Pacific Ring of Fire and experiences frequent seismic activity. At this magnitude and depth, shaking would have been felt across the Port-Vila area, though early reports suggest the overall impact level was relatively low. A small number of residents reported feeling the tremor.

No tsunami warning information was included in the available source material. Authorities and residents in the region are likely monitoring conditions as details continue to emerge.
events.prayer: Residents of Vanuatu shaken by a magnitude 6 earthquake with emerging impact still being assessed

events.title: Earthquake in Solomon Islands 05/19/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.8 earthquake struck about 83 kilometers west-northwest of Kirakira in the Solomon Islands on May 19, 2026. The quake originated about 82 kilometers underground, placing it in the deeper range where shaking is typically spread over a wider area but felt less intensely at the surface.

No damage or injuries have been reported at this time. At this depth, the risk of severe surface damage is generally lower than with shallow quakes, though residents in the region may still have felt noticeable shaking.

The Solomon Islands sit in one of the world's most seismically active zones, and earthquakes of this size are not uncommon in the area. Local communities are accustomed to regular seismic activity, but each event serves as a reminder of the region's vulnerability.
events.prayer: Residents of the Solomon Islands shaken by a magnitude 5 earthquake with no reported injuries yet

events.title: Flood in China
events.body:
Heavy rain struck central and eastern China over the weekend, triggering dangerous flooding across several provinces. In the southwestern region of Guangxi, a pickup truck carrying 15 passengers fell into a flooded river, killing four people and leaving six others missing. Rescue teams were searching for the missing as more heavy rain was expected to move eastward and southward over the following days.

Also in Guangxi, a magnitude 5 earthquake struck near the city of Liuzhou, killing two people and forcing more than 7,000 residents to evacuate. Authorities released emergency relief supplies to help with both the flood and earthquake response.

In Hubei province, the city of Jingzhou declared an emergency and suspended work and business activities. Nearby Yichang saw its heaviest single-day rainfall in 36 years. Officials warned of growing risks from flash floods, river flooding, and waterlogged cities, and closed tourist sites and outdoor school activities as a precaution.
events.prayer: Millions of residents across China facing deadly flooding, flash floods, and earthquake damage amid ongoing severe storms

events.title: Other in Mexico
events.body:
A new report from the World Meteorological Organization warns that record-breaking heat, deadly floods, worsening drought, and stronger hurricanes are putting millions of people across Latin America and the Caribbean at serious risk. The findings point to growing threats to food supplies, safe water access, and stable housing across the region.

Communities are facing a combination of disasters at once — some areas baking under extreme heat while others are overwhelmed by flooding or dry spells that are lasting longer than usual. Hurricanes in the region are also intensifying, adding to the danger.

The report underscores that these are not isolated events. Across the region, ordinary people are struggling to meet basic needs as weather extremes become more frequent and more severe. Aid organizations and local communities are under increasing pressure to respond.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Millions across Mexico and Latin America facing intensifying heat, floods, drought, and hurricanes threatening food and water security

events.title: Earthquake in United States 05/18/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 4.9 earthquake struck in the waters about 168 kilometers east of Atka, Alaska on May 18, 2026. The quake was shallow, occurring about 10 kilometers below the surface, which can cause stronger shaking to be felt at the ground level compared to deeper earthquakes.

Atka is a small, remote community located in the Aleutian Islands, a seismically active chain of islands stretching across the North Pacific. The area sits along one of the world's most active earthquake zones.

No damage reports or injuries were mentioned in the available information. Given the earthquake's moderate size and its location well offshore from an already sparsely populated area, widespread impact is considered limited.
events.prayer: Residents of remote Alaska near an offshore earthquake zone following a shallow magnitude 4.9 tremor

events.title: Earthquake in China — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A 5.2-magnitude earthquake struck the Guangxi region in southern China, killing two people and leaving one person missing. At least 13 buildings collapsed in the quake, which hit in the early morning hours. The two people killed were an elderly couple, a man in his sixties and a woman in her fifties.

More than 7,000 residents were evacuated from the affected area. Search and rescue teams were still working to find the missing person as of that morning.
events.prayer: the families of those killed, the one person still missing, and the more than 7,000 people displaced from their homes

events.title: Earthquake in China — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A 5.2-magnitude earthquake struck Liuzhou city in China's Guangxi region early Monday, killing two people and leaving one person missing. Thirteen buildings collapsed, and over 7,000 residents were evacuated from the affected area.

The two confirmed dead were an older couple. Rescue workers, including search dogs and teams with earthmovers, combed through the rubble looking for the missing person. Video from state media showed people running out of high-rise buildings and damaged homes reduced to piles of debris.

Search and rescue efforts were still underway as of Monday morning. Earthquakes are not unusual in China — just last January, a much stronger quake in the remote Tibet region killed at least 126 people and damaged thousands of buildings.
events.prayer: the two who died, the one still missing, and the more than 7,000 people displaced from their homes

events.title: Earthquake in China — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5 earthquake struck the Guangxi region of southwest China early Monday morning, killing two people and forcing more than 7,000 residents in the city of Liuzhou to leave their homes. Buildings collapsed, and search and rescue teams are working to find anyone still trapped.

Authorities have warned that transport routes in the area face disruptions, which could slow the movement of supplies and emergency crews. Evacuated residents are waiting as officials assess which buildings are safe to return to.
events.prayer: Thousands of displaced residents in China sheltering after a deadly earthquake with ongoing rescue efforts

events.title: Earthquake in China 05/18/2026 — Magnitude 5
events.body:
A magnitude 5.2 earthquake struck Liuzhou, in China's southern Guangxi region, bringing down more than a dozen buildings and killing at least two people. More than 7,000 residents have been evacuated from the area as rescue teams work through the damage.

Search and rescue operations are ongoing as workers look for anyone still trapped in the rubble. The scale of building collapses in a moderate-sized earthquake like this points to significant impact on local neighborhoods and communities.
events.source_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

events.title: Other in Sudan
events.body:
Sudan is facing one of the worst hunger crises in the world, with nearly 20 million people struggling to find enough food. The United Nations has warned that more than 800,000 children could suffer severe malnutrition this year. Parts of the country are edging closer to famine.

The crisis is being driven by ongoing civil war, which has forced massive numbers of people from their homes and caused health and food systems to collapse across the country. Communities that were already vulnerable are now at extreme risk.

Aid agencies are calling urgent attention to the scale of suffering, as conditions continue to deteriorate and access to basic necessities remains out of reach for millions of Sudanese families.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories
events.prayer: Nearly 20 million people in Sudan facing severe hunger, famine risk, and collapsed food systems amid civil war

events.title: Drought in Somalia
events.body:
At least six million people in Somalia are going days without enough food, according to United Nations aid teams. Of those, nearly two million are young children described as being at high risk of illness or death. Aid workers are warning that Somalia is at real risk of famine.

The crisis has been made worse by the fallout from conflict in the Middle East, which has disrupted international aid and funding. Families across the country are struggling to find enough to eat, and the situation is especially dangerous for the youngest and most vulnerable.

UN teams are raising the alarm and calling for urgent attention to the growing hunger emergency before conditions worsen further.
events.source_title: UN News - Global perspective Human stories

Conditions in the Philippines can change quickly during flood events, and continued monitoring is needed as the situation develops.

events.body:

The tremor was centered in the Pardis area, east of the capital, at a shallow depth of about 10 kilometers. Shaking was also felt as far as Karaj, a suburb roughly 40 kilometers to the west, where residents were alarmed but unharmed. Magnitude estimates varied slightly between sources, ranging from 4.3 to 4.6.

Authorities confirmed no injuries or destruction were linked to the event. Despite the lack of damage this time, the incident is a reminder of Iran's position in one of the world's most earthquake-prone regions, where two tectonic plates meet beneath the country.

Past earthquakes in Iran have been devastating. A 1990 quake killed more than 40,000 people, and a 2003 earthquake near Bam claimed over 26,000 lives. Shallow quakes and aging buildings have historically made the country especially vulnerable when stronger tremors strike.

events.title: Flood in United States 04/20/2025
events.body:
On April 20, an unmanned self-driving car operated by Waymo drove onto a flooded road in San Antonio, Texas, and was swept into a creek. The vehicle was empty at the time, and no injuries were reported. Waymo's San Antonio service has been temporarily suspended while the company works on a software fix.

In response, Waymo issued a voluntary recall covering nearly 3,800 of its robotaxis across the United States. The company said safety is its top concern and that it has already taken steps to limit vehicle access to areas where flash flooding could occur.

The incident has drawn wider attention to the limits of self-driving technology. Experts note that the boundaries of what autonomous vehicles can handle safely often only become clear when something goes wrong, and that more incidents like this are likely as these vehicles become more common on public roads.

events.title: Volcano in Indonesia 05/02/2025
events.body:
A volcanic eruption at Mount Dukono on Halmahera island in North Maluku, Indonesia, killed three hikers — two Singaporeans and one Indonesian — on Friday morning. A group of 20 hikers, a mix of Singaporeans and Indonesians, had climbed to the summit despite a ban on climbing permits and a restricted zone extending four kilometers from the crater, both in place since mid-April. The volcano had erupted more than 200 times since late March.

The eruption struck quickly, with two blasts occurring within about a minute of each other. The second blast sent volcanic rock and debris down the mountainside. A large boulder crushed two hikers who had turned back to help an injured member of the group. A third hiker was found dead near the summit the following day.

Rescue teams worked through the weekend to recover the victims from beneath heavy volcanic ash. The surviving hikers were treated at a nearby hospital, and the Singaporean survivors have since returned home.
events.prayer: Families in Indonesia and Singapore grieving hikers killed by a sudden volcanic eruption
